title: Moroccan Chickpea Harira Soup Bowl
description: A light and flavorful soup bowl featuring roasted Moroccan spices, served with a side of crispy nuka-pickled onions.

ingredients.name: Moroccan spice blend
ingredients.notes: available at most Middle Eastern markets or online

instructions: Toss the chopped onion with 1 tablespoon olive oil, salt, and pepper on a baking sheet. Roast in the oven for 20 minutes, or until caramelized.
In a large pot, heat the remaining 1 tablespoon olive oil over medium heat. Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the Moroccan spice blend, ground cumin, smoked paprika (if using), and roasted onion to the pot. Cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the chickpeas, vegetable broth, and sage to the pot. Bring to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 20 minutes.

While the soup simmers, prepare the nuka-pickle. Slice the onion thinly and place it in a bowl. Mix 1 cup water with 1 cup nuka (see notes) and pour it over the sliced onion. Let it sit for at least 30 minutes to allow the flavors to meld.
To serve, ladle the hot soup into bowls and top with a dollop of nuka-pickle, chopped fresh parsley (if using), and a sprinkle of sea salt.
Note: this recipe is for refrigerator pickling/preserving only — store in the refrigerator and consume within 1 week. Do not water-bath can without an acid (vinegar or lemon juice).
